Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible nephropathy. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria) may indicate renal tubular dysfunction or possible diabetic involvement, though blood glucose is normal. Low serum globulin and alkaline phosphatase are noted but of unclear clinical significance. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend further renal evaluation and correlation with clinical context.
